title: LG Display (LPL) to Release Quarterly Earnings on Thursday - MarketBeat

title: Analysis: How TSMC avoids memory's boom-and-bust cycle
content: Ahead of TSMC's earnings call, DIGITIMES senior analyst Luke Lin explained that TSMC typically does not revise its full-year revenue forecast or capital expenditure during its first-quarter earnings announcement. If adjustments are needed, TSMC usually waits until July. That is when second-quarter results and third-quarter guidance are released, giving the company a firmer basis for any revisions.

title: Naura revenue climbs on China chip tool demand, margins tighten amid rising costs and competition
content: China semiconductor equipment maker Naura Technology delivered strong 2025 revenue growth on rising chip tool demand and domestic substitution, but weaker margins and intensifying competition signal a tougher next phase.

title: Global wafer foundry industry and status, 2026: AI-driven growth and fierce competition
content: Global wafer foundry revenue in 2026 is estimated to grow 23.5% year-over-year, expected to reach US$250 billion.

title: South Korea's DeepX moves DX-M1 AI chip into mass production; builds up inventory to address supply constraints
content: South Korean AI semiconductor startup DeepX has entered mass production and supply for its in-house neural processing unit (NPU), the DX-M1, as it seeks to scale revenue amid tightening wafer capacity and rising memory costs. The company said it has secured more than 30 purchase orders and is targeting US$40 million in revenue in 2026, supported by demand for low-power, cost-efficient AI chips.

title: Omdia: AMOLED smartphone display shipments are expected to decline by 7% in 2026
content: Omdia says that AMOLED smartphone display shipments are expected to decline by 7% in 2026 amid rising memory prices and increasing market uncertainty. Both flexible and rigid OLED shipments will decline.

title: Samsung Unveils ‘Design Is an Act of Love’ Exhibition at Milan Design Week 2026
content: Samsung Electronics today announced the opening of its “Design is an Act of Love” exhibition at Milan Design Week 2026. title: Samsung Launches ‘Raise the Bar’ Campaign With Joe Cole To Upgrade UK Pubs’ TV Experience
content: Samsung Electronics has launched the “Raise the Bar” campaign in the U.K., partnering with former England international football player, Joe Cole to support local pubs with upgraded TV technology ahead of a major summer of football. The initiative aims to enhance the match-day viewing experience by providing selected venues with Samsung TVs and promotional support, […]
